title: NOC 33103 – Pharmacy Technical Assistants and Pharmacy Assistants | GTR Immigration
description: Pharmacy Technical Assistants and Pharmacy Assistants under NOC 33103 provide essential support services within community and hospital pharmacies across Canada. As prescription volumes continue to grow and healthcare systems expand, this TEER 3 occupation remains in steady demand nationwide. For internationally trained candidates, NOC 33103 may qualify under Express Entry and selected Provincial Nominee Programs, offering a structured pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER3 – Occupations usually require college training or on-the-job trainingMajor Group33 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesSub-Major Group331 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesMinor Group3310 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesNOC Code33103

duties: Pharmacy Technical Assistants and Pharmacy Assistants perform some or all of the following duties:
Review prescriptions for completeness and accuracy
Enter patient and prescription information into databases
Assist pharmacists with preparing, packaging, and labeling medications
Compound oral solutions, ointments, and creams
Stock shelves and price pharmaceutical products
employment_requirements: Completion of up to two years of a Pharmacy Technical Assistance vocational program
Completion of a six- to twelve-month Pharmacy Assistant college programOR
Unlike Pharmacy Technicians (NOC 32124), formal national certification is generally not required, though employer-based training standards may apply.
International applicants may require credential assessment depending on employer and province.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $28,500 to $57,300, depending on province, employer, and experience.
job_outlook: Candidates with vocational training may experience stronger employment opportunities.
labour_market_demand: Pharmacy Technical Assistants and Pharmacy Assistants are employed in:

This steady demand is supported by expanding pharmaceutical services and aging population healthcare needs. Strong employment prospects are found in provinces such as Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, and Quebec.
Why Immigrate to Canada as a Pharmacy Assistant?
TEER 3 eligibility under Express Entry (program dependent)
Opportunities in retail and hospital pharmacy environments
Career progression toward certified Pharmacy Technician roles
Pharmacy support roles remain vital to Canada’s medication distribution and patient care systems.
pathways: If you are qualified under NOC 33103, you may be eligible for immigration pathways to Canada through Express Entry or Provincial Nominee Programs.
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your pathway toward permanent residence in Canada under NOC 33103.
